# msgscope <!-- version -->

**msgscope** is a read-only structural-integrity / tamper inspector for Outlook
`.msg` files. It inspects the raw MS-OXMSG / Compound File Binary container and
reports **indicators** — each with a severity, a confidence, and the evidence
(which stream, which byte offset, observed vs expected). It never outputs a
verdict; a human examiner draws the conclusion.

Read the background article: <!-- ARTICLE_URL -->

## Downloads

Standalone single-file executables (no Python required):

- `msgscope-linux-x86_64`
- `msgscope-macos-arm64`
- `msgscope-windows-x86_64.exe`

Or install for pipelines: `pipx install msgscope`.

## Verify your download

Every asset is covered by `SHA256SUMS`, generated by CI. A file-integrity tool
should let you verify its own integrity:

```console
# macOS / Linux
shasum -a 256 -c SHA256SUMS

# Windows (PowerShell)
Get-FileHash .\msgscope-windows-x86_64.exe -Algorithm SHA256
```

> The Windows binary is **not** code-signed, so SmartScreen may warn on first
> run. Verify the SHA-256 hash above before running.
